global_context.scene_description: A full-frame UI mockup that fills the entire image — designed to look exactly like an Uber surge-pricing map of Phoenix, but the surge is HVAC emergency demand. NO phone bezel, NO hand, no real-world context — just the map UI filling the frame edge-to-edge. The map shows a stylized aerial view of Phoenix metro, with neighborhood labels softly visible: 'PHOENIX', 'SCOTTSDALE', 'TEMPE', 'MESA', 'CHANDLER', 'GLENDALE'. Across the map, RED surge zones are glowing in concentrated patches over the populated neighborhoods, with a heatmap-style red-orange-yellow gradient. The red zones are clearly active and pulsing. At the top of the screen, a prominent banner reads 'EMERGENCY HVAC SURGE — 1.5x ACTIVE' in bold red on white. A subtitle below reads 'Demand has spiked. Phoenix is hot.' At the bottom-right of the map, a small floating callout/popup reads 'Estimated wait: 3-5 days · $14,500 average'. The map uses Uber/Lyft-style aesthetic — clean light grey roads, dark grey background, prominent red surge overlay. Mood: that 'oh shit' moment when you open Uber on New Year's Eve and see the surge map applied to HVAC service. Editorial UI mockup, NOT photo, NOT cartoon.
